examples.title: Value Iteration Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to compute optimal value function and policy in an MDP
examples.explanation: Value Iteration iteratively applies Bellman optimality update until convergence. It computes V* and then derives π*. Time: O(|S|² |A|) per iteration. Space: O(|S|).

examples.title: Policy Evaluation
examples.description: Computing the value function for a given policy
examples.explanation: Policy Evaluation computes V^π for a fixed policy π by iteratively applying the Bellman equation. It is the first step in Policy Iteration.

examples.title: Policy Iteration Algorithm
examples.description: Finding optimal policy by alternating evaluation and improvement
examples.explanation: Policy Iteration alternates between computing V^π (evaluation) and improving π using V^π (improvement). It converges to optimal policy in finite MDPs. Often faster than Value Iteration despite more iterations.
examples.timeComplexity: O(|S|² × |A| × iterations) per iteration, typically fewer iterations than Value Iteration

examples.title: Simple Grid World MDP Setup
examples.description: Defining an MDP for a simple 4x3 grid world problem
examples.explanation: This sets up a classic grid world MDP. States are grid positions. Actions have stochastic outcomes (intended move 80%, slip 20%). Rewards: +1 for goal, -1 for pit, small negative for each step (to encourage shortest path).
